size: varies: toy (under 10 lb), miniature (10-15 lb), standard (40-70 lb)

exercise.details: Standard Poodles need 1-2 hours of vigorous exercise daily — they are athletic retrievers at heart. Miniature Poodles need 45-60 minutes. Toy Poodles need 30-45 minutes. All varieties enjoy swimming, fetch, agility, and obedience training. Mental stimulation through puzzle toys and training sessions is equally important.

grooming.details: Poodles have a single-layer curly coat that does not shed but grows continuously and mats quickly if not maintained. Without regular brushing (every 1-2 days) and professional clipping every 4-6 weeks, coats become severely matted. This is the most grooming-intensive aspect of Poodle ownership. Ear hair must be plucked or cleaned regularly to prevent infections.

diet.daily_calories_adult: standard: 1200-1800 kcal; mini: 500-700 kcal; toy: 200-350 kcal
diet.notes: Feed a high-quality diet appropriate for size and activity level. Standard Poodles can be prone to bloat — use slow-feed bowls and avoid exercise near mealtimes. Toy Poodles are prone to hypoglycemia as puppies — feed small, frequent meals. All sizes benefit from omega-3 supplementation for coat health.

health.vet_checkups: annually; eye and hip testing recommended for standards
health.notes: Poodles are generally a healthy long-lived breed. Sebaceous adenitis (a skin condition) is more common in Standards — an annual skin punch biopsy can detect it early. Addison's disease (adrenal insufficiency) is twice as common in Poodles as other breeds.

temperament.trainability: exceptional — ranked #2 most intelligent dog breed
temperament.notes: Poodles are highly intelligent, sensitive, and eager to please. They excel in obedience, agility, and any dog sport. They can be sensitive to stress and harsh handling — positive reinforcement training is essential. Toy Poodles can be prone to anxiety and excessive barking without proper socialization.

care_summary: Poodles are highly intelligent, low-shedding dogs that require committed grooming — professional clipping every 4-6 weeks is non-negotiable. Their intelligence demands mental enrichment daily. They are versatile athletes suitable for families and individuals alike, with the Toy and Miniature varieties adapting well to apartment living.
